review_notes: The functions accurately map to Kubernetes security controls related to system and application access control as per ISO27001 A.9.4. The naming conventions are mostly followed, but there is room for improvement in clarity and specificity.
naming_improvements: Consider adding more specificity to 'k8s_apiserver_authentication_enabled' to indicate the type of authentication (e.g., 'k8s_apiserver_authentication_rbac_enabled')
title: System and Application Access Control
step1_reasoning: The ISO27001 A.9.4 control focuses on system and application access control, which maps well to Kubernetes RBAC for managing user and service account permissions. Ensuring no cluster-admin bindings and enforcing least privilege are direct equivalents. Disabling automatic token mounting for service accounts reduces unnecessary access. Additionally, ensuring API server authentication is enabled aligns with access control requirements.
